findings.message: Generated Windows player process started successfully from EscapeFromNightmares/Build/Windows/EscapeFromNightmares.exe.
Player log was created and includes Unity engine initialization, Input System initialization, physics fallback initialization, NullGfxDevice creation, and MonoManager assembly reload start.
Log scan found no Exception, Error, Crash, Failed, Missing, NullReference, or ArgumentException entries.

The generated player did not exit on its own within the 15-second smoke window and was terminated by the validation script; this is expected for an interactive player but means this was a startup smoke check, not a full run-completion check.
The headless log stops early during assembly reload, so a visible interactive launch or automated player-side quit hook is still recommended for deeper validation.

followups: Add concrete automated Stage1 smoke tests in Unity PlayMode.
Add an optional generated-player smoke quit hook or command-line smoke mode for deterministic player startup validation.
Run an interactive visible-player playtest for final hit-area, map marker, and monster/hiding balance tuning.
